vision: The network monitor a serious operator would actually use
platform: macOS via Tauri v2 (Rust + React + TypeScript)
delivery: GitHub → GitHub Actions CI → .dmg download from Releases

stories.title: Tauri Rust backend — system network data
stories.description: src-tauri/src/main.rs: Implement Tauri commands: get_network_stats() returning real bytes/sec per interface using sysinfo crate, get_active_connections() using std::process::Command netstat, get_ip_info() calling ip-api.com, get_download_speed() timing a 5MB Cloudflare fetch. All as async Tauri commands callable from frontend.

stories.title: React frontend — single screen layout
stories.description: Replace src/App.tsx with the full single-screen layout per DESIGN_SYSTEM.md. Three zones. Import Liquid Glass + Metal buttons from components/. All data fetched via Tauri invoke() calls to Rust backend.

stories.title: Design components — from prompt library
stories.description: Create src/components/ui/LiquidGlassButton.tsx, MetalButton.tsx, SpeedGauge.tsx (SVG arc), SpeedGraph.tsx (canvas real-time line). All from the 21st.dev design prompt library already provided.

stories.title: GitHub Actions CI — Mac build pipeline
stories.description: Create .github/workflows/build-mac.yml that: on push to main, runs on macos-latest, installs Rust + Tauri deps, runs npm run tauri build, uploads the .dmg as a GitHub Release artifact. This is the delivery mechanism.
